reviews.review_title: It's absolutely wonderful for a demo
reviews.review_description: I barely ever write reviews for games, but this one was just such a fresh breath of air. From the multitude of interesting mechanics, which I've barely seen on vr games at all to interesting graphics, it's been a wonder playing the demo. The lack of proper levels honestly isn't even much of a problem due to the many unique waves you can create with enemies at the current moment. Although, one small gripe I do have with this game is the guns. This might be simply a skill issue on my end, but I've found them to be of not much use or even in the way at times. Even with this, I do feel like this game has incredible potential and could maybe even one day become one amongst the legendary must play games on vr. With that, all I can say is to keep up the amazing work dev! Can't wait to see the future updates!

reviews.review_title: great demo, suffers from one major flaw
reviews.review_description: i’ll just say it, we all have this game because it’s ultrakill’s vr clone, and it’s a pretty solid one at that. only problem is this game has an ammo system, and the reason ultrakill didn’t have one is very apparent throughout the game. you have to either choose between wanting to shoot your gun or parry, not do both like you could in ultrakill, and it really bogs down the game. on the topic of parrying, i think whatever you parry should have a sort of aim-assist built in to direct it closer to whatever shot at you as aiming it in vr is nearly impossible without the AOE it has

I loved the levels, I was so excited for the full game, sandbox mode doesn’t feel like vitriol. 
Please give us new levels!

I loved the old movement before the revamp, however the new movement mechanics seem a little worse. The new air grab mechanic seems less responsive than the original jumping and dashing, and the grappling hook seems to have also gotten an overhaul making it seem almost slippery and less controllable. I don’t really like the new systems personally but the game does have some great potential still.

reviews.review_title: Gang good for what it is
reviews.review_description:
It's a free game made in unity, so don't expect a gameplay or vosual masterpiece, but as a demo for a bigger project, its amazing. 

Controls can be odd, the button layout a bit weird, parying and reloading finicky, but if you practice and get good, combat can flow very nicely. I can't wait to see new levels, enemies, and especially weapons, I'm hooked and can't wait to see this taken further.
So much fun

Look, people complain about some things like oh I can't parry and shoot, but who cares it's a amazing experience and I love your take on a vr ultrakill keep up the good work btw the dark parts of the middle levels creeped me out a little and that's amazing as I Said keep up the good work peace out

reviews.review_title: Haven’t Even Started and I Love It
reviews.review_description: You know what? I haven’t even shot a gun and I love it. This game does ultrakill in vr better than actual ultrakill vr. The physics are so good as well.

It's been a while since I last played a VR game that actually FEELS like a videogame. It's amazing.

You play as a gunslinging robo-preacher in a western/medieval/steampunk demon-infested world?!? It sounds metal because it IS METAL.

The late-90s style is perfect for Standalone VR, and the soundtrack sets the tone just right.

10/10 already, I can't wait to see what comes next!

Request to the Developer: Consider implementing IOBT! It would be sick to elbow strike some demons in VR, especially if a blade/spike could extend from the elbow, like the claws extend from your hands!

reviews.review_title: Really cool but hard to play
reviews.review_description: This seems like a really cool game but there arr some things that aren't clear or are difficult to do such as the double jump off walls, another difficult thing is the revolver's hammer gets stuxk and won't let mr shoot, maybe I'm missing something but it isn't explained.

There are plenty of bugs, and i got stuck in a certain part of the level because of one. the physics system itself causes a lot of jank, specifically with the knights. The spinning revolver mechanic is also a little janky and unpolished it feels, i couldn’t get it to do a full spin because it would jerk back and forth, but it would charge up the same— just not satisfying. reloading the revolver is also messy because it’s physics is active while the chamber rolls out, meaning you might have to click the reload button a few times before it successfully comes out. The lack of a sprint is also disappointing, since it makes common traversal a little more boring. the default walking speed is really slow. though you could barely call it movement tech, dashing into a jump is the most reliable form of quick movement, though the air time can get you caught on a wall. i love the concept, it’s great, and the right applications of it can make it a good game. the state it’s in is just not the best optimized for moving fast and being chaotic, because the level design is cramped at times, and the previous complaints i had. overall, it’s pretty fun and a good experience
Absolutely beautiful,even tho it’s a demo it’s still has multiple mechanics,weapons,mobility and levels and they’re all amazing.Can not wait for the full version

reviews.review_title: Almost amazing but one tiny thing
reviews.review_description: Game is great, has some challenge to it, but reloading is genuinely annoying and kind of detracts. Especially for lefties. If y’all removed reloading, it would make parrying better, movement more fun as you aren’t paying attention to the frankly janky revolver physics (as if you are moving it shuts immediately), it allows much more freedom. Thank you for your time.

Okay I don’t even know how to start. This game is just sooooooo good. The art is amazing and the atmosphere is PERFECT. It’s terrifyingly surreal. You get the feeling you could turn the corner and watch the game unfold into a horror game. 🧑‍🍳👌. And the combat… oh my gosh. The parrying makes you fell like freaking Superman. The slight freeze followed by a huge explosion… Meta hire this man. And the guns… it’s like a cold glass of water on a long summer day. First off the gunplay in this game is top notch. The aim is dead on. And the gun spin… oh boy is it amazing. The feeling of charging up a spin before opening a door and unleashing the power of twelve suns upon a single enemy is beautiful. The puzzles are peak. The combat is peak. And the movement is and say it with me now… PEAK. And it’s only a demo! Please everyone reading this Please please PLEASE get this game. It’s free and it shows the dev that they should make more. I will pay my soul and more for this game to release for real. This has the most potential I have seen in a LONG time. Sir who made this I tip my hat to you good man. Keep up the good work. And soon you may go VITRIOL.

reviews.review_title: Great, but with potential to be greater
reviews.review_description:
I want to preface this review by saying that I love how fluid and open the movement feels, and the two accessible weapons so far are awesome. That being said, there are a few places this game could improve.
1. Reloading
   Reloading is really, really hard on the move, especially if you're fighting something (also, I'm left-handed, so reloading the revolver is awkward as heck). I'd recommend a system similar to Hellsweeper wherein you can manually reload (or do the trick reload like with the shotgun) for a damage bonus, or you can do an automatic reload for no damage bonus
2. Parry
   The parry system is cool, but it feels a little clumsy. What makes the parry feel clumsy is the lack of a way to direct or aim your parry. For this, I would recommend a system that directs the parry to the direction the player is looking, or add some sort of aim assist to the parry
3. Rebinding
   Just let us rebind our controls freely, that's all
4. Walljumping
   The walljumping system is great and feels awesome when you do it, but the problem is in actually doing it. I'd recommend letting us grab the wall.
With all of that said, I cannot emphasize how much I love this game, and I do truly want it to grow into something beautiful.
Peak bro
